label: Minor tongue bite without deep laceration or infection red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor tongue bite injury.
No deep laceration requiring repair, uncontrolled bleeding, dental/facial trauma pathway, infection, airway symptoms, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Deep laceration or repair needed, uncontrolled bleeding, dental/facial trauma source, airway symptoms, deep-space swelling, infection concern, severe pain, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound/oral surgery plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dental/oral surgery if instructed, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports basic cuts and wound-care self-care, keeping wounds clean, clinician-directed wound treatment, and escalation for infection, bleeding, retained foreign body, or deep-structure concern. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed minor oral soft-tissue bite injuries with deep laceration, uncontrolled bleeding, airway, infection, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports basic cuts and wound-care self-care, keeping wounds clean, clinician-directed wound treatment, and escalation for infection, bleeding, retained foreign body, or deep-structure concern. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed minor oral soft-tissue bite injuries with deep laceration, uncontrolled bleeding, airway, infection, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of minor tongue bite
